scope_note: QA Fermat Quotient Parallel Structure cert [430]. Corrects an imprecise closing claim in cert [429] (no WSS->Wieferich implication exists). Primary sources: Wieferich, A. (1909) 'Zum letzten Fermat'schen Theorem' Journal fuer die reine und angewandte Mathematik 136 pp. 293-302 doi:10.1515/crll.1909.136.293 (defines 2^(p-1)=1 mod p^2 criterion); Meissner, W. (1913) (first known Wieferich prime 1093); Beeger, N.G.W.H. (1922) (second known Wieferich prime 3511); Eisenstein, F.G.M. (1850) (Fermat quotient q_p(a)=(a^(p-1)-1)/p mod p, general definition); Sun, Z.H. and Sun, Z.W. (1992) 'Fibonacci and Lucas numbers and powers of five' Acta Arithmetica 60 pp. 371-388 doi:10.4064/aa-60-4-371-388 (shows WSS and Wieferich are PARALLEL independently-necessary conditions for a hypothetical FLT first-case failure, not equivalent or implicative to each other); McIntosh, R.J. and Roettger, E.L. (2007) 'A search for Fibonacci-Wieferich and Wolstenholme primes' Math. Comp. 76 pp. 2087-2094 doi:10.1090/S0025-5718-07-01955-2 (no WSS prime below 9.7e14). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (C1) FLT baseline: 2^(p-1)=1 mod p for all odd primes p in [3,5000]; 669/669 PASS. (C2) Fermat quotient q_p(2)=(2^(p-1)-1)/p mod p is well-defined in [0,p-1] for all odd primes in [3,5000], computed via pow(2,p-1,p*p) without materializing the full big integer 2^(p-1); 669/669 PASS. (C3) Wieferich primes (q_p(2)=0) in [3,5000] are exactly {1093,3511}, matching the historical record (Meissner 1913, Beeger 1922); exact match PASS. (C4) Independence check: the two known Wieferich primes 1093 and 3511 are NOT Wall-Sun-Sun primes -- F_{alpha(p)} != 0 mod p^2 for both, computed via fast doubling; 2/2 PASS. CORRECTS: cert [429]'s closing note speculated a WSS->Wieferich implication; this cert documents the correct relationship as structural PARALLEL (both are p^2-layer vanishings of a Fermat-quotient-style residue; both independently necessary, not equivalent, not implicative) per Sun & Sun (1992), and C4 gives direct empirical evidence of independence (Wieferich primes are not WSS). CONNECTS TO CHAIN: [428] (F_{p-(5/p)}=0 mod p, FLT-analog depth-1 baseline) + [429] (WSS depth-2 criterion, none known) + [430] (powers-of-2 depth-2 analog via Wieferich, parallel not implicative). THEOREM NT: all pure integer; pow(2,p-1,p*p) and fast doubling stay integer; no float, no observer layer at all. 4 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-15.
